clusters.title: Elderly man allegedly murdered with metal pole in unprovoked attack
clusters.neutral_headline: Elderly man allegedly murdered with metal pole in unprovoked attack
bullet_summary: Reported by The Age, 9News, ABC Just In

items.title: Elderly man allegedly murdered with metal pole in unprovoked attack
items.standfirst: A 36-year-old man is accused of going on a violent rampage through a rural Queensland town, attacking three strangers.
items.content: A 36-year-old man is accused of going on a violent rampage through a rural Queensland town, attacking three strangers.

items.title: Man charged with murder after allegedly killing man in seemingly random rampage
items.standfirst: The series of alleged violent attacks took place over about eight minutes in the small town
items.content: A man has been charged with murder after allegedly killing a man and assaulting two others south-west of Bundaberg in a series of alleged violent attacks.The rampage took place over about eight minutes in the small town of Childers, leaving an elderly man dead after he was allegedly brutally bashed with a metal pole.This allegedly happened after a 36-year-old man, driving a Subaru Liberty, deliberately crashed into a Mitsubishi Pajero.LIVE UPDATES: 'Blow 'em up': Trump's shock threat to friendly nation over straitWhen the Pajero driver got out of his car after the collision, it is alleged that the offender reversed into him, before punching the other man multiple times in the face.After this, the 36-year-old drove onto Whietbridge Road in North Isis, where he confronted a 78-year-old man, allegedly punching him in the head before hitting him with a metal pole.The North Isis man suffered critical injuries and died at the scene.The Subaru driver then allegedly drove to Crescent Street in Childers, where he allegedly assaulted a 66-year-old man.NATIONAL: Australia's richest people - and how they amassed their fortunes - revealed Earlier, the alleged offender from Bundaberg West was allegedly involved in a car crash on the Bruce Highway at Glenorchy, where he allegedly deliberately reversed into a Tesla, before verbally abusing the driver of the Tesla.None of the victims are believed to be known to the man.Members of the public then restrained the man, allowing police to arrest him.He has been charged with one count each of murder, attempt to murder, acts intended to maim, disfigure or disable, dangerous operation of a vehicle and evasion.He will appear at Gladstone Magistrates Court via video link today.

items.title: Man charged with murder after several people attacked in regional Queensland
items.standfirst: A 36-year-old man has been charged with murder after allegedly attacking several people south of Bundaberg.
items.content: A 36-year-old man has been charged with murder after allegedly attacking several people south of Bundaberg.

clusters.title: Nine-months' pregnant nursery owner chases down alleged plant thief
clusters.neutral_headline: Nine-months' pregnant nursery owner chases down alleged plant thief

items.title: Nine-months' pregnant nursery owner chases down alleged plant thief
items.standfirst: She managed to recover plants worth hundreds of dollars.
items.content: A heavily pregnant nursery owner has chased down a thief at her Adelaide business, stopping him from stealing plants worth hundreds of dollars.Footage shows the thief yesterday pushing a trolley full of plants from Exotic Botanic in the city's north-east into a carpark.Moments later, the owner who is nine months pregnant, runs after him.READ MORE: Australia's top 10 richest people revealedShe managed to regain the trolley and pushed it back to the store.The nursery later took to social media to explain what happened and appeal for thieves to stop."Please don't let me chase you while I'm nine months pregnant," the owner said."And, oh, please don't steal hundreds of dollars' worth of plants from us."Police say they have not received any reports from the incident.

items.title: Pregnant nursery owner chases down alleged thief
items.standfirst: A nine-months pregnant plant store owner has chased down an alleged thief in Adelaide.
items.content: A nine-months pregnant plant store owner has chased down an alleged thief in Adelaide.
